extracted.brandBackground: Seniors Helping Seniors offers medical and non-medical personal and home health services provided primarily by seniors to other seniors.

leadership.name: Kiran Yocom
leadership.background: Co-founded Seniors Helping Seniors and has served as Chairwoman since February 2006.
leadership.whyItMatters: Provides long-term brand vision and continuity since inception.

leadership.name: Philip W.S. Yocom
leadership.background: Has served as CEO since October 2006 and previously served as President.
leadership.whyItMatters: Directs the overall strategic growth and executive leadership of the system.

leadership.name: Namrata Yocom-Jan
leadership.background: Appointed President in December 2020 after serving as Executive Vice President since May 2016.
leadership.whyItMatters: Responsible for day-to-day operational leadership and franchise relations.

leadership.name: Daniel Jan
leadership.background: Appointed Chief Operating Officer in June 2020 after serving as VP of Operations since April 2007.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees operational support, training, and system compliance.

item19.notes: Item 19 provides detailed breakdowns of gross revenues for full-time, part-time, and current model cohorts, as well as by years in business.

lineItems.notes: Initial franchise fee for a single unit.
Mandatory training fee paid to the franchisor.
Estimated rent and security deposit for 3 months; zero if home-based.
Deposits for local utilities; zero if home-based.
Office furniture and equipment; zero if home-based.
Cost of signage; zero if home-based.
General office supplies and computer hardware.
Mandatory marketing spend during the first 3 months.
Travel, lodging, and meals for training; zero if virtual.
Professional fees for establishing the business.
Recommended employee salaries for the first 3 months.
Working capital for the first 3 months.

hiddenCosts.name: National Convention Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Mandatory annual fee per person, whether or not you attend.

hiddenCosts.name: Late Reports Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Charged per month for any late submission of required documents.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: Disputes must be resolved by mediation and/or litigation only in Pennsylvania.

operationalRisks.title: Spousal Liability
operationalRisks.description: Spouses must sign a personal guarantee making them liable for all franchise obligations.

operationalRisks.title: Sales Performance Required
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must maintain minimum sales performance levels or risk losing territory rights or termination.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Payments
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must make minimum royalty or advertising fund payments regardless of sales levels.

extracted.conceptRationale: The franchise provides medical and non-medical personal care, home health, and companion services for seniors.
extracted.staffingRationale: The business model requires hiring caregivers and office staff to coordinate and deliver home care services.

riskReasons: 2 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.

midCohort.label: Average Annual Revenue - Excludes 1st and 2nd Year Owners (2025)

source.label: Average Annual Revenue - Excludes 1st and 2nd Year Owners (2025)

underwriting.assessment: $250,000 liquid covers the $125,588 mid-point build-out without financing, though that leaves little working-capital cushion. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $94,718/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: The franchise provides medical and non-medical personal care, home health, and companion services for seniors.

benchmark.label: Health & wellness (med-spa, IV, longevity, recovery)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Licensed clinical labor plus regulatory/insurance load and equipment depreciation. These concepts frequently disclose NO Item 19, so benchmarks matter most precisely where extraction gives you the least.
considerations: Product/consumables run ~15–25%; clinical/licensed labor is expensive at ~25–35%.
Factor heavy equipment depreciation, malpractice/liability insurance, and regulatory compliance — none of it is in the FDD.
With little or no Item 19, validate revenue per treatment room and ramp directly with Item 20 franchisees.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 12–25% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: conventionally staffed · ≈ 7–9.8 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Health & wellness (med-spa, IV, longevity, recovery)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Taken from the FDD (~$1,788/mo); already netted in margin after fees & rent.
Estimated at the 15–25% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 25–35% conventionally staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor's statements don't show the distress signals we screen for — positive net worth and a clean audit. Still worth a quick confirmation against current figures.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Derived from the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
